articles.title: Jackdaw boss warns of winter fuel shortages if gas field not approved
articles.summary: Adura says the UK government must approve North Sea production urgently to avoid domestic supply shortages.
articles.titleZh: 寒鸦老板警告称，如果气田不获批准，冬季燃料将短缺
articles.summaryZh: 阿杜拉表示，英国政府必须紧急批准北海生产，以避免国内供应短缺。

contentParagraphs.en: Image caption, Adura chief executive Neil McCulloch says the UK would have limited options in the event of "a gas supply emergency"ByScotland editorUpdated 11 minutes agoThe owners of the Jackdaw gas platform in the North Sea say it is "hyper critical" that the UK government approves production to avoid the risk of domestic supply shortages this winter.Speaking to BBC News at the field 150 miles east of Aberdeen, Adura chief executive Neil McCulloch said the project was in its final stages and could meet 6% of the UK's gas needs from 1 October.The industry regulator is considering revised applications for production at Jackdaw, and Adura's Rosebank oil field west of Shetland, after a court ruled that both had been unlawfully approved.Environmental campaigners say this summer's deadly and record-breaking heatwaves demonstrate the need to tackle climate change by rejecting both projects.Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, Campaigners in London last week urge Andy Burnham not to open the North Sea to new projectsBut McCulloch said with only about eight days of gas storage, the UK would have limited options in the event of "a gas supply emergency."He said this could come in the form of a prolonged period of still, cloudy weather which impeded the generation of wind and solar power, or hostility from "foreign threat actors".BBC News has been given exclusive access to Jackdaw which is undergoing final checks and tests to be ready for production in the event of government approval being granted.The "business-as-usual" atmosphere is somewhat surreal given the uncertainty hanging over the project, which has so far cost around £1.5bn according to Aberdeen-based Adura, a joint venture between Shell and the Norwegian state energy firm Equinor.Figure caption, Jackdaw gas field is 150 miles off the coast of AberdeenMcCulloch said: "If I were the secretary of state for energy security and net zero, I'd be looking closely at where's my next source of energy security, and you're standing on it."The wells are drilled, they're hooked up. We're just readying the systems. It will be ready for the 1st of October."Jackdaw will play a vital part of this winter's gas supply," he added, providing energy security, employment and taxation to the UK.Environmentalists say Jackdaw will only produce 2% of the country's annual gas demand during the lifetime of the field."It would be a huge betrayal of the British public for the UK government to approve new oil and gas fields at a time when ordinary people are suffering so much as a result of these record-breaking heatwaves," said Tessa Khan, executive director of the campaign group, Uplift.In response McCulloch said: "So we all watch the same news, and we see that. "But what we're saying is that Jackdaw should not take that on its shoulders, or it should take a very small portion of that. "It's a very, very small proportion of the total global emissions."As Andy Burnham prepares for Downing Street, he is under pressure from within the Labour party to allow more oil exploration and to lower and stabilise taxation on investment and production.Former prime minister Sir Tony Blair, trade union leaders, and Westminster's energy committee are among those echoing US President Donald Trump's call for the UK to "open up the North Sea."That is a challenge. The most accessible and most lucrative fields have largely been drained.Production in the basin peaked in 1999 at 4.5 million barrels of oil (or equivalent) per day (BOE). In 2024 it produced just over one million BOE.The Energy Transition Institute, external at Robert Gordon University predicts that current policies will lead to some 1,600 offshore job losses per year for the coming decade. "Oil and gas is declining faster than many of us were expecting, but the renewables industry is simply not ready to take all the jobs," the institute's director, Prof Paul de Leeuw, told me.Rosebank contains an estimated 300 million to 500 million barrels of oil, making it the largest known untapped field in UK waters.Adura says the gas from Jackdaw could supply 1.4m homes.Decisions on oil and gas licensing are a matter for the UK government at Westminster but the Scottish government, run by the pro-independence Scottish National Party, also takes an interest, and it is split on the subject.Oil was once central to the economic case for Scotland leaving the UK but, as first ministers, the SNP's Nicola Sturgeon and Humza Yousaf both opposed Rosebank and Jackdaw.Current First Minister John Swinney has tried to fudge the matter, saying new developments should only go ahead if they are compatible with the UK's commitment to reducing greenhouse gas emissions.Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, Climate campaigners launched a legal challenge against Jackdaw and Rosebank Last year, the Court of Session in Edinburgh ruled that both fields had been unlawfully approved because the consenting process had failed to take into account the impact on the climate of burning the oil and gas extracted from them.The judge Lord Ericht said the operators would have to submit revised environmental impact assessments to the regulator, the North Sea Transition Authority (NSTA).On Tuesday, Adura said the updated assessment it had been required to produce suggested Jackdaw would account for less than 0.02% of annual global greenhouse gases during its lifetime.That claim was dismissed as "self-serving" by Greenpeace, whose UK chief scientist Doug Parr said approving the field would be "reckless and indefensible" in the context of international commitments to slow down global warming.If the NSTA gives its approval, the final decisions will fall to the current Energy Secretary, and Burnham's potential chancellor, Ed Miliband.More than any other Labour politician, in opposition and in government, Miliband has crafted a policy which is positive about renewable energy such as wind, wave and solar and sceptical of new oil and gas developments.While Miliband has sai
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片说明，Adura首席执行官尼尔·麦卡洛克表示，如果发生“天然气供应紧急情况”，英国的选择有限。苏格兰编辑11分钟前更新。北海Jackdaw天然气平台的所有者表示，英国政府批准生产以避免今年冬天国内供应短缺的风险“非常重要”。在阿伯丁以东150英里的油田接受BBC新闻采访时，Adura首席执行官尼尔·麦卡洛克表示该项目已进入最后阶段，从 10 月 1 日起可满足英国 6% 的天然气需求。法院裁定 Jackdaw 油田和设得兰群岛以西的 Adura 罗斯班克油田均获得非法批准后，行业监管机构正在考虑修改生产申请。环保人士表示，今年夏天致命且破纪录的热浪表明有必要拒绝这两个项目来应对气候变化。图片来源，盖蒂图片社图片说明，上周伦敦的环保人士敦促安迪·伯纳姆 (Andy Burnham)但麦卡洛克表示，由于只有大约八天的天然气储存，英国在发生“天然气供应紧急情况”时的选择有限。他说，这可能会以长时间多云的天气（阻碍风能和太阳能发电）或来自“外国威胁行为者”的敌意的形式出现。英国广播公司新闻已获得寒鸦号的独家报道，该号正在接受最后的检查和测试，一旦获得政府批准即可投入生产。考虑到该项目笼罩着不确定性，“一切如常”的气氛有点超现实。据总部位于阿伯丁的 Adura（壳牌与挪威国有能源公司 Equinor 的合资企业）称，该项目迄今为止已耗资约 15 亿英镑。 Jackdaw 气田距离阿伯丁海岸 150 英里，McCulloch 表示：“如果我是负责能源安全和净零排放的国务卿，我会密切关注我的下一个能源来源在哪里。”能源安全，而你就站在它上面。”井已经钻好，它们已经连接起来。我们刚刚准备好系统。将于10月1日准备就绪。他补充道，“寒鸦将在今年冬季的天然气供应中发挥重要作用，”为英国提供能源安全、就业和税收。环保人士表示，在该气田使用寿命期间，寒鸦只能生产该国每年天然气需求的2%。“在普通民众因这些破纪录的石油和天然气田而遭受巨大痛苦的时候，英国政府批准新的油气田将是对英国公众的巨大背叛。” Uplift 活动组织执行董事泰莎·汗 (Tessa Khan) 表示：“所以我们都看同样的新闻，我们都看到了这一点。但我们想说的是，寒鸦不应该承担这一责任，或者应该承担其中的一小部分。” “这在全球排放总量中所占的比例非常非常小。”当安迪·伯纳姆为唐宁街做准备时，他面临着来自工党内部的压力，要求他允许更多的石油勘探，并降低和稳定投资和生产的税收。前首相托尼·布莱尔爵士、工会领导人和威斯敏斯特能源委员会都响应美国总统唐纳德·特朗普呼吁英国“开放北海”的呼吁。这是一个挑战。最容易开采和最有利可图的油田大部分已被抽干。该盆地的产量在 1999 年达到峰值，达到每天 450 万桶石油（或当量）（BOE）。到 2024 年，其产量将超过 100 万桶油当量。罗伯特戈登大学外部能源转型研究所预测，当前政策将导致未来十年每年约 1,600 个离岸岗位流失。该研究所所长 Paul de Leeuw 教授告诉我：“石油和天然气的下降速度比我们许多人预期的要快，但可再生能源行业还没有准备好抢走所有工作岗位。”罗斯班克估计蕴藏着 3 亿至 5 亿桶石油，使其成为英国水域已知最大的未开发油田。阿杜拉表示，来自寒鸦的天然气可以供应 140 万户家庭。石油和天然气许可的决定由英国政府负责。威斯敏斯特，但由支持独立的苏格兰民族党领导的苏格兰政府也对此感兴趣，并且在这个问题上存在分歧。石油曾经是苏格兰离开英国的经济案例的核心，但作为首席部长，苏格兰民族党的尼古拉·斯特金和胡姆扎·优素福都反对罗斯班克和寒鸦。现任首席部长约翰·斯温尼试图回避此事，称新的发展只有在符合英国对英国的承诺的情况下才应该继续进行。图片来源，盖蒂图片社 图片说明，气候活动人士对 Jackdaw 和 Rosebank 发起了法律挑战。去年，爱丁堡法院裁定，这两个油田的批准都是非法的，因为同意程序没有考虑到燃烧从其中提取的石油和天然气对气候的影响。法官埃里克特勋爵表示，运营商必须向监管机构北海过渡管理局 (NSTA) 提交修订后的环境影响评估。周二，阿杜拉表示，更新后的环境影响评估它被要求进行的评估显示，寒鸦在其生命周期内所产生的全球温室气体不足0.02%。这一说法被绿色和平组织斥为“自私”，绿色和平组织的英国首席科学家道格·帕尔表示，在减缓全球变暖的国际承诺的背景下，批准这一领域将是“鲁莽和站不住脚的”。如果国家技术局批准，最终决定将由现任能源部长和伯纳姆的潜在财政大臣艾德做出。米利班德。无论是在野党还是政府，米利班德制定的政策对风能、波浪能和太阳能等可再生能源持积极态度，但对新的石油和天然气开发持怀疑态度。

articles.title: How beachgoers are turning snaps into science
articles.summary: Scientists hope to learn more about the impact of climate change on coasts through people's photos.
articles.titleZh: 海滩游客如何将快照变成科学
articles.summaryZh: 科学家希望通过人们的照片更多地了解气候变化对海岸的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: Image caption, CoastSnap is part of a project called Co-creating Coastal Resilience (CoCor)ByAgriculture and environment correspondent, BBC News NIBeachgoers are being invited to become citizen scientists to help track how our coastline is changing.Armed with nothing more than a smartphone, visitors to Tyrella Beach in County Down can now take part in a global project by snapping a photo through a fixed frame and logged via a website.Each picture becomes part of a growing record that scientists will use to monitor how the beach shifts over time, helping them understand the effects of coastal erosion, rising sea levels and climate change.Open days will be held to show people how to get involved.As a geomorphologist with Geological Survey Northern Ireland, Melanie Biausque studies how coastlines change over time.She is leading the project in Northern Ireland and said people who regularly visit the beach have the power to transform scientists' understanding of how it is changing."We're trying to understand how the coastline changes now and how it will evolve in the future, what can we do now to protect it so it's not too impacted by the changes that are going to happen in the environment."Image caption, CoastSnap cradle on Tyrella beach Why Tyrella?The shifting sands at Tyrella beach are home to the first CoastSnap photo spot in Northern Ireland.CoastSnap, which began in Australia, is part of a project called CoCor - Co-creating Coastal Resilience.Tyrella was chosen because of the work local volunteers have already carried out to restore its sand dunes.Biausque first learned about their work after seeing a presentation to Newry, Mourne and Down council, where volunteers spoke of how they had collected seeds to plant new marram grass, and installed fencing and signage to protect the seedlings."When I heard that, I realised that I had never had any idea that was happening - and I really wanted to put that on the map and to say, things are happening," she said."But one thing that we don't do is to see how that evolves."Image caption, The photos will help scientists analyse how and why material is deposited in certain olacea and what effect that has on the coastHow is the coastline changing?Coasts are designed to move - waves, tides, currents and wind are always shifting sand around, while dunes grow and erode naturally over time.However, Biausque and other scientists are preparing for "big" changes, in the form of sea level rise and more frequent storms due to the effects of climate change."If we want to understand how it's going to happen and what's going to happen on the coast, we need to understand what is happening now and how it works."That is where CoastSnap comes in.By comparing multiple photos taken from exactly the same position over months and years, researchers can build up a detailed picture of how the coastline responds to different weather conditions and seasons.The Northern Ireland branch of the project is funded by the Department for the Economy, with more sites across soon to be equipped with the photo cradle and signs to explain how to take part in the project.'Easy-peasy'Taking part is simple - the only equipment needed is your phone.Just one specific part of the coastline will be photographed, so that the team has many pictures of one spot that can form a database of evidence to be studied.A steel cradle, designed to hold your phone horizontally, has been placed on a fence post.It holds your phone in exactly the right position, ensuring every photo is taken from the same angle.An opening in it frames the specific area that the team are interested in - the upper part of the beach where the volunteers carried out their work.After taking the photo, visitors simply scan the QR code on the sign below and upload it to the CoastSnap website.Image caption, Signs and protective fencing are in place to try to give the new grass seedlings the best chanceBiausque said the process is entirely anonymous."You don't have to leave your name or anything, unless you want to."The project will run throughout the seasons and there are options on the page to record what else you notice."If you're coming in the winter and you can see coastal erosion or you can see vegetation change, you can click on those and you can add comments - though you don't have to," she added.While people like Biausque cannot visit Tyrella every day, regular visitors can help the scientists answer their questions by sending photos."Why do we have algae standing here? Why sometimes you can see the vegetation progressing? Why do you come in the winter and the beach is so different?"All of this is the interaction between the sand or the sediment and the waves, the currents, the tides, and at the minute we know that all of those processes are going to change in the future."But to understand how we can live with it and adapt around that, we need to understand better how it works today. So that's why we need to collect."

articles.title: Low-e windows keep homes cool … but may set neighbours’ property on fire
articles.summary:
Low-emissivity windows also keep houses warm in winter, but use on bowed glass can have magnifying-glass effect
Low-emissivity or low-E window glass is a useful green technology for keeping buildings warm in winter and cool in summer … but a rare side-effect can set the neighbour
articles.titleZh: 低辐射窗户使房屋保持凉爽……但可能会导致邻居的财产着火
articles.summaryZh:
低辐射率窗户也能让房屋在冬天保持温暖，但使用弓形玻璃会产生放大镜效果
低辐射率或低辐射窗玻璃是一种有用的绿色技术，可以使建筑物冬暖夏凉……但一种罕见的副作用可能会影响邻居

contentParagraphs.en: Low-emissivity or low-E window glass is a useful green technology for keeping buildings warm in winter and cool in summer … but a rare side-effect can set the neighbours’ property on fire.The glass is coated with a thin layer of metal or metal oxide which lets visible light through but acts like a mirror in the infrared. Heat from the interior is reflected back in, retaining warmth in winter, while unwanted solar radiation is repelled in summer.But that reflected heat must go somewhere, which may be a problem if the glass is slightly bowed. The curved window acts like a magnifying glass, focusing heat on a spot or along a line. There have been instances when concentrated reflected sunlight has scorched wooden decking, melted artificial turf and damaged plants.In one extreme case, a series of fires was traced to the neighbours’ newly fitted low-E windows. After three fires in one week, the affected homeowner moved a propane tank that was close to the focal point.skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionSeveral high-profile cases mean architects now know to avoid the curved reflecting glass that can produce “skyscraper death rays” in larger buildings. But the small domestic version may be less obvious, as any low-E window could potentially be a firestarter if it is poorly manufactured.
contentParagraphs.zh: 低辐射率或低辐射窗玻璃是一种有用的绿色技术，可以使建筑物冬暖夏凉……但一种罕见的副作用可能会导致邻居的财产着火。这种玻璃涂有一层薄薄的金属或金属氧化物，可以让可见光透过，但在红外线中就像一面镜子。来自内部的热量被反射回来，在冬天保持温暖，而在夏天则排斥不需要的太阳辐射。但是反射的热量必须到达某个地方，如果玻璃稍微弯曲，这可能会成为问题。弧形窗口就像放大镜一样，将热量集中在一个点或沿着一条线。在某些情况下，集中反射的阳光会烧焦木质地板、融化人造草坪并损坏植物。在一个极端的案例中，一系列火灾是由邻居新安装的低辐射窗户引起的。一周内发生三起火灾后，受影响的房主移动了靠近焦点的丙烷罐。几个引人注目的案例意味着建筑师现在知道要避免使用曲面反射玻璃，这种玻璃会在较大的建筑中产生“摩天大楼死亡射线”。但小型家用版本可能不太明显，因为任何低辐射窗户如果制造不良，都可能成为火灾隐患。

articles.title: How can I get air conditioning in my home and how much does it cost?
articles.summary: As summers in the UK get hotter, is it time for air conditioning to become a permanent feature in most homes?
articles.titleZh: 我怎样才能在家里安装空调以及需要多少钱？
articles.summaryZh: 随着英国的夏天越来越热，空调是否已经成为大多数家庭的永久配备了呢？

contentParagraphs.en: Costs range widely depending on what is wanted and/or required.Portable units are the cheapest form of air con, ranging from £350 to £650 on average, depending on the brand and performance, according to Checkatrade., externalHowever, as demand has soared in recent weeks some retailers began selling the cooling machines for £149, as Lidl did in its infamous middle aisles.Wall mounted or split air con units can cost between £750 and £1,100 each, Checkatrade says - but that is just the unit, and does not include the labour and other installation costs, such as hooking it up to the property's electricity fuse board. Installation company Heatable suggests, external a full cost is typically £2,000 to £3,500, but can go up to £6,000 if you want to have it in more than one room.Ducted air con systems cost the most, between £990 and £1,750 without installation costs, according to Checkatrade. Fitting the ducting or remedial work to hide it inside properties means it is likely to be more expensive than any of the other systems given the level of work involved. Heatable estimates it to be between £5,000 and £10,000, depending on the property size, layout and how complex the ductwork needs to be.The size of both split and ducted units are determined by what is known as the BTU (British Thermal Unit), Checkatrade says, to ensure it will cool the space it's required to. The larger the BTU number, the bigger the room to cool, and therefore the more expensive the unit.Following installation, consumer group Which? suggests the running costs "vary wildly" and depend on the type of system. "A typical portable air conditioner adds roughly 25p to 40p an hour to your electricity bill," it says.Stating the obvious, the main benefit of having air con in a home is that it can keep it cool during times of extreme heat, meaning people can sleep, work and generally live more comfortably.However, air con units require electricity, the price of which remains much higher now than it was a few years ago, and so you can expect your overall energy bill to increase if you purchase and use one regularly.Some environmental groups have also raised concerns over the impact of such technology on the environment, including the chemicals used in units to cool air. Portable air con units are easy to set up, use and move around homes. They are cheaper to buy than the alternatives and do not require professional installation. But they can be more expensive to run due to not being as energy efficient. Checkatrade says portable or window units are a good short-term solution and are also ideal for renters or those who live in listed buildings who cannot make major changes to their property.Split systems are much better at cooling rooms and are also cheaper to run, according to Checkatrade. They are also quieter than portable units, but they do entail larger upfront costs and need to be installed by a professional tradesperson.Ducting air con is the least noticeable option due to the vents and can cool multiple rooms. But when it comes to retrofitting older homes it can be complex due to having to find space for the equipment, which adds to installation costs.
contentParagraphs.zh: 成本范围很广，具体取决于想要和/或要求的内容。根据 Checkatrade 的数据，便携式空调是最便宜的空调形式，平均从 350 英镑到 650 英镑不等，具体取决于品牌和性能。然而，随着最近几周需求猛增，一些零售商开始以 149 英镑的价格出售制冷机，就像 Lidl 在其臭名昭著的中间过道中所做的那样。壁挂式或分体式空调每台的价格在 750 英镑到 1,100 英镑之间， Checkatrade 表示，但这只是单位，不包括劳动力和其他安装成本，例如将其连接到酒店的电力保险丝板。安装公司 Heatable 建议，外部全部成本通常为 2,000 至 3,500 英镑，但如果您想在多个房间安装，则可能高达 6,000 英镑。据 Checkatrade 称，管道式空调系统成本最高，在 990 至 1,750 英镑之间（不含安装成本）。安装管道或进行补救工作以将其隐藏在房产内意味着考虑到所涉及的工作水平，它可能比任何其他系统都更昂贵。 Heatable 估计其成本在 5,000 英镑到 10,000 英镑之间，具体取决于房产规模、布局以及管道系统所需的复杂程度。Checkatrade 表示，分体式和管道式机组的尺寸均由所谓的 BTU（英国热力机组）决定，以确保其能够冷却所需的空间。 BTU 数字越大，冷却空间就越大，因此设备就越贵。安装后，消费者群体是哪一个？表明运行成本“差异很大”并且取决于系统的类型。报告称：“典型的便携式空调每小时会增加大约 25 便士到 40 便士的电费。” 显而易见，家里安装空调的主要好处是，它可以在酷热时保持凉爽，这意味着人们可以更舒适地睡眠、工作和生活。然而，空调需要电力，现在的价格比几年前要高得多，因此，如果您定期购买和使用空调，您的整体能源费用预计会增加。一些环保组织还对此类技术对环境的影响表示担忧，包括用于冷却空气的装置中使用的化学品。便携式空调装置易于安装、使用和在家中移动。它们比其他替代品更便宜，并且不需要专业安装。但由于能源效率不高，它们的运行成本可能更高。 Checkatrade 表示，便携式或窗式空调是一种很好的短期解决方案，对于租房者或住在受保护建筑中且无法对其财产进行重大改变的人来说也是理想的选择。据 Checkatrade 称，分体式系统在冷却房间方面效果更好，而且运行成本也更低。它们也比便携式装置更安静，但它们确实需要更大的前期成本，并且需要由专业技工安装。由于通风口，管道空调是最不引人注目的选择，并且可以冷却多个房间。但在改造旧房屋时，由于必须为设备寻找空间，这可能会很复杂，这会增加安装成本。

articles.title: UK heatwave spreads further north and west as temperatures continue to climb
articles.summary: This year's third heatwave is one of the longest lasting heatwaves since the infamous year of 1976.
articles.titleZh: 随着气温继续攀升，英国热浪进一步向北和向西蔓延
articles.summaryZh: 今年的第三次热浪是自臭名昭著的 1976 年以来持续时间最长的热浪之一。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Anadolu via Getty ImagesByJennifer BartramBBC WeatherTemperatures are expected to reach 35C (95F) on Thursday as the current heatwave spreads further north and west across the UK. The year's third heatwave, which began earlier this week, is not expected to break new temperature records, but the longevity of the heat will make it feel remarkable.On Wednesday, temperatures peaked at 33.8C at Heathrow Airport in the strong July sunshine with Wales recording 31.2C at Cardiff Bute Park, Scotland seeing 29.0C at Aboyne and 27.1C at Helen's Bay in Northern Ireland.From Thursday and Friday more areas are likely to see temperatures climb up to the high 20s or low 30s. Image caption, Above-average heat across much of the country on Thursday is set to spread westwards and northwardsHow hot will it get?The highest temperatures of this heatwave are expected on Thursday and Friday.High pressure across the UK will keep conditions dry and settled for most over the next two days, allowing the heat to build extensively. However, temperatures are not expected to reach those recorded in June's heatwave, where a provisional 37.7C was recorded in Lingwood, Norfolk. This time, maximum daily temperatures will reach 34C across parts of London and Cambridgeshire on Thursday, with some areas maybe reaching 35C. Image source, BBC WeatherImage caption, Temperatures on Thursday will be 34C across southern and eastern England, with some areas reaching 35CBy Friday, the area of high pressure will shift further northwards, allowing the heat to spread further north and west into parts of southern Wales and the Midlands, with temperatures in the low to mid 30s. At the same time, a developing easterly breeze will bring slightly cooler conditions to more eastern areas. For Northern Ireland and Scotland, temperatures are expected to reach the mid-20s Celsius by Friday.Amber heat health alerts are in place across parts the Midlands, eastern and southern England until 12 July.Yellow heat health alerts are also in force across northern England for the same period.This means that significant impacts are likely across health and social care services, including a rise in deaths, particularly among people aged 65 and over or who have health conditions.On Wednesday, the National Fire Chiefs Council (NFCC) said the dry and hot weather meant there was an increased risk of wildfires, and advised people to avoid using disposable barbecues in open countryside, park and moorlands.Where is there a heatwave at the moment?The extensive and prolonged heat means that many places are already experiencing an official heatwave. A heatwave is declared when certain temperature thresholds are met - or exceeded - for three consecutive days or more. These temperature thresholds are: 28C or more across London and the Home Counties27C across south-east England26C across central England and south-east Wales25C for the rest of England, Wales and all of Northern Ireland and ScotlandParts of the south and east of England officially declared a heatwave on Monday and have seen temperatures reach 30C or higher for the last five days.Other areas which are in official heatwave conditions include parts of the West Midlands, southern and south-west England.Image source, AFP via Getty ImagesImage caption, Spectators at Wimbledon had to cope with the heatWhen will the heatwave end?Although temperatures will peak on Thursday and Friday, there will not be a quick cool down with many places hanging on to the heat through the weekend and much of next week.Many areas could meet or exceed the heatwave criteria somewhere in the UK for the next 10 days: that is the rest of this week, the weekend and all of next week too.After Sunday the temperatures may start to dip below 30C, but will still remain well above the July average of 18-23C.This makes it one of the longest lasting heatwaves since the infamous year of 1976.Back then, England saw multiple locations record 13 to 16 consecutive days above 30C.The highest recorded July temperature in the UK was recorded in 2022 at Coningsby, Lincolnshire.Will there be another heatwave?Forecasters issued their three-month summer outlook on 1 June which suggested higher-than-normal chances of hotter weather during June.Above-average temperatures are expected for each month of July and August, and "significant bursts" of heat are expected in the UK, and across Europe.MeteoGroup, who currently provide BBC Weather with data, point to a "few notable high temperature spikes".According to the Met Office, having a hotter summer is now twice as likely than the period of 1991-2020, which is consistent with our warming climate.

articles.title: Who is Zeynep Sonmez, the Wimbledon with the ‘Palestine’ watermelon racket?
articles.summary: Turkish tennis player Zeynep Sonmez defies a Wimbledon ban to stand in solidarity with Palestine.
articles.titleZh: 拥有“巴勒斯坦”西瓜球拍的温网选手 Zeynep Sonmez 是谁？
articles.summaryZh: 土耳其网球运动员泽伊内普·桑梅斯不顾温网禁令，声援巴勒斯坦。

contentParagraphs.en: Zeynep Sonmez‘s 2026 Wimbledon singles campaign came to a halt in the second round following a 7-5, 6-3 defeat to American Claire Liu.But the 24-year-old Turkish tennis player left an indelible mark on the tournament by taking a quiet yet powerful stance for Palestine, on the iconic grass courts of London.Prevented by tournament organisers from displaying a pro-Palestinian solidarity brooch on her attire, Sonmez creatively attached a watermelon-themed vibration dampener on her racket strings.The watermelon has become a symbol of Palestinian solidarity and pride among activists after Israel suppressed displays of the Palestinian flag. The watermelon not only has the same colours as Palestine’s flag – red, white and black – but the fruit is also one of the most popular crops for farmers across Palestine.Speaking to Turkiye’s Anadolu agency shortly after her match, Sonmez exposed the bureaucratic hurdles she faced and what she perceived as a glaring double standard from international tennis administrators regarding political expression.Zeynep Sonmez of Turkiye, who uses a watermelon-shaped vibration dampener on her racket, and Jessica Bouzas Maneiro of Spain play Shuko Aoyama of Japan and En-Shuo Liang of Taiwan in the Ladies Doubles competition during day four of The Wimbledon Tennis Championships at All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club on July 2, 2026, in London, UK [Ray Tang/Anadolu]“I used to wear a brooch, but tournaments no longer allow me to wear it,” she said. Sonmez pointed out that tennis authorities have not barred athletes from displaying the Ukrainian flag in solidarity with Kyiv during the war with Russia.“I argued with the managers that the Ukrainian flag is allowed, but the Palestinian one is not. Following our conversations, they stated that they strictly would not permit it,” she added.Faced with a strict prohibition on her clothing accessories, Sonmez has adopted the watermelon symbol as a permanent fixture on her racket.She said tournament officials could not object to her specific equipment choices, and her quiet defiance drew praise back home in Turkiye.Turkish Youth and Sports Minister Osman Askin Bak publicly applauded her show of solidarity amid the humanitarian tragedy in Palestine and said this showed how sports can be used to promote universal human values.Omer Celik, spokesperson for Turkiye’s governing Justice and Development Party (AKP), also praised Sonmez for keeping human dignity alive amid Israel’s ongoing genocidal war on Gaza, which has killed at least 73,000 Palestinians since October 7, 2023.Sonmez expressed immense gratitude for the overwhelming wave of national support, emphasising to Anadolu that this backing made her feel as though she were never alone on the court.From Istanbul ballgirl to the ‘golden racket’Beyond the recent political spotlight, Sonmez is authoring an unprecedented chapter in Turkish sports history. Born in Istanbul on April 30, 2002, and with deep familial roots in the Black Sea coastal town of Arhavi, Sonmez’s journey to the upper echelons of tennis has been built on long-term dedication.Her greatest dreams were ignited a decade ago when she worked as a humble ballgirl at a Women’s Tennis Association (WTA) tournament in Istanbul. Watching her compatriot Cagla Buyukakcay secure a historic title on home soil served as the ultimate inspiration for the young athlete.Recognised early for her vast potential, local Turkish sports media confidently dubbed her the “golden racket of the future” as early as 2016. She has spent 15 years turning that prediction into reality, eventually earning the prestigious Athlete of the Year award at the Harper’s Bazaar Turkiye Women of the Year ceremony in Istanbul following her breakthrough 2025 season.EASTBOURNE, ENGLAND – JUNE 25: Jelena Ostapenko of Latvia celebrates following victory against Zeynep Sonmez of Turkiye during the Women’s Singles Quarter Final on Day Four of the Lexus Eastbourne Open at Devonshire Park on June 25, 2026 in Eastbourne, England [Charlie Crowhurst/Getty Images for LTA]Guided by her current coaches, Issam Jellali and Mehmet Bayraktar, Sonmez’s meteoric rise was prompted by crucial technical adjustments and immense mental resilience. A critical turning point in her career took place after a heartbreaking Wimbledon qualifying defeat to Ukrainian player Daria Snigur.Following this profound loss, Sonmez intentionally adopted a much more aggressive, net-rushing style to perfectly complement her famously strong movement and agility on the court.Throughout her rapid ascent, she has fiercely emphasised the values of hope, relentless focus, and pure sportsmanship. This unwavering commitment to integrity was famously demonstrated during a notable fair-play moment at a tournament in Monastir. During a tense match, she and her opponent Eva Lys remarkably corrected line calls in each other’s favour, a rare display of mutual respect that earned her widespread admiration across the professional tour.Rewriting the Turkish tennis record booksHer recent campaigns reflect a player operating at the absolute peak of her career. Sonmez has accumulated $625,019 in career prize money, registering a highly competitive win/loss record of 24 wins and 15 losses across 30 competitive matches this calendar year alone. Her tactical evolution has translated into historic, record-breaking milestones on the international tour.She claimed her first career WTA 250 singles title at the hard-court event in Merida, Mexico, in 2024, dominating American Ann Li 6-2, 6-1 in a swift 70-minute final.With continued strong showings across the globe, including a second-round appearance at the prestigious WTA 1000 event in Rome, Sonmez shattered Buyukakcay’s previous national ranking record of No 60. She officially became the highest-ranked Turkish tennis player in WTA history, climbing past the Top 60 to reach her current career-high rank of No 51 in the world.Sonmez has repeatedly proven her formidable ability to c
contentParagraphs.zh: 泽伊内普·桑梅兹 (Zeynep Sonmez) 的 2026 年温布尔登单打比赛在第二轮以 7-5、6-3 输给美国人克莱尔·刘 (Claire Liu) 后陷入停顿。但这位 24 岁的土耳其网球运动员在伦敦标志性的草地球场上为巴勒斯坦采取了安静而有力的立场，在赛事中留下了不可磨灭的印记。在以色列压制巴勒斯坦国旗的展示后，西瓜已成为巴勒斯坦活动人士团结和自豪的象征。西瓜不仅与巴勒斯坦国旗的颜色相同（红、白、黑），而且还是最受巴勒斯坦农民欢迎的农作物之一。赛后不久，桑梅兹在接受土耳其阿纳多卢机构采访时，揭露了她所面临的官僚障碍，以及她认为国际网球管理者在政治表达方面明显的双重标准。土耳其选手泽内普·桑梅兹（Zeynep Sonmez）使用西瓜形状的减振器2026 年 7 月 2 日，英国伦敦全英草地网球和门球俱乐部举行温布尔登网球锦标赛第四天，女双比赛中，西班牙选手杰西卡·布扎斯·马内罗 (Jessica Bouzas Maneiro) 与日本选手青山修子 (Shuko Aoyama) 和台湾选手梁恩硕 (En-Shuo Liang) 对阵 [Ray Tang/Anadolu]“我曾经佩戴胸针，但比赛不再允许我佩戴它，”她说。索梅兹指出，网球当局并没有禁止运动员在与俄罗斯的战争期间展示乌克兰国旗，以声援基辅。“我与经理们争辩说，乌克兰国旗是允许的，但巴勒斯坦国旗是不允许的。在我们谈话后，他们表示他们绝对不会允许这样做，”她补充道。面对着对她的服装配件的严格禁止，索梅兹采用了西瓜标志作为她球拍上的永久固定装置。她说赛事官员不能反对她的具体要求土耳其青年和体育部长奥斯曼·阿斯金·巴克 (Osman Askin Bak) 公开称赞她在巴勒斯坦人道主义悲剧中表现出的团结，并表示这表明了如何利用体育来促进普世人类价值观。土耳其执政党正义与发展党 (AKP) 发言人奥马尔·塞利克 (Omer Celik) 也赞扬桑梅兹在以色列对加沙正在进行的种族灭绝战争中保持了人类尊严，这场战争至少造成了死亡自 2023 年 10 月 7 日以来，共有 73,000 名巴勒斯坦人。桑梅兹对全国压倒性的支持浪潮表示了极大的感谢，并向阿纳多卢强调，这种支持让她感觉自己在球场上从来不孤单。从伊斯坦布尔女球童到“金球拍”，除了最近的政治聚光灯之外，桑梅兹正在谱写土耳其体育史上前所未有的篇章。桑梅斯于 2002 年 4 月 30 日出生于伊斯坦布尔，在黑海沿岸小镇阿尔哈维有着深厚的家族根基，她的网球高层之旅是建立在长期奉献的基础上的。十年前，当她在伊斯坦布尔的女子网球协会 (WTA) 锦标赛中担任一名不起眼的球童时，她最大的梦想被点燃。看着她的同胞卡格拉·布尤卡卡伊 (Cagla Buyukakcay) 在家乡获得历史性冠军，这对这位年轻运动员来说是终极的鼓舞。土耳其当地体育媒体早在 2016 年就因其巨大潜力而被认可，自信地称她为“未来的金球拍”。她花了 15 年的时间将这一预测变为现实，最终在伊斯坦布尔举行的《时尚芭莎》土耳其年度最佳女子颁奖典礼上获得了久负盛名的年度运动员奖。英国伊斯特本 - 6 月 25 日：2026 年 6 月 25 日在英国伊斯特本举行的雷克萨斯伊斯特本公开赛第四天的女单四分之一决赛中，拉脱维亚选手耶莱娜·奥斯塔彭科 (Jelena Ostapenko) 庆祝战胜土耳其选手 Zeynep Sonmez [Charlie Crowhurst/Getty Images for LTA]在伊萨姆·杰拉利（Issam Jellali）和穆罕默德·拜拉克塔（Mehmet Bayraktar）教练的带领下，桑梅斯的迅速崛起得益于关键的技术调整和巨大的心理韧性。她职业生涯的一个关键转折点发生在令人心碎的温布尔登预选赛输给乌克兰选手达里亚·斯尼古尔之后。在这次惨重的失利之后，桑梅斯有意采用一种更具侵略性、冲网的风格，以完美地补充她在球场上著名的强劲运动和敏捷性。在她的快速崛起过程中，她强烈强调希望、不懈专注和纯粹的体育精神的价值观。这种对诚信坚定不移的承诺在莫纳斯提尔锦标赛的一个著名的公平竞赛时刻得到了著名的体现。在一场紧张的比赛中，她和她的对手伊娃·利斯显着地纠正了对方的判罚，这是一种罕见的相互尊重的表现，为她在整个职业巡回赛中赢得了广泛的钦佩。重写了土耳其网球纪录。她最近的竞选活动反映了一位球员在职业生涯的绝对巅峰时期的表现。 Sonmez 的职业生涯奖金累计达到 625,019 美元，仅在今年的 30 场比赛中就取得了 24 胜 15 负的激烈胜负记录。她的战术演变已转化为国际巡回赛上历史性、破纪录的里程碑。2024 年，她在墨西哥梅里达举行的硬地赛事中夺得职业生涯第一个 WTA 250 单打冠军，并在 70 分钟的决赛中以 6-2、6-1 战胜美国选手安·李。凭借在全球范围内持续强劲的表现，包括在罗马著名的 WTA 1000 赛事中进入第二轮，桑梅兹的表现彻底崩溃了。布尤卡卡伊此前的全国排名记录为第60位。她正式成为WTA历史上排名最高的土耳其网球运动员，突破前60名，达到她目前职业生涯最高的世界排名第51位。桑梅兹一再证明了她强大的能力。

articles.title: Outcry as Meta lets users make AI images from public Instagram profile pics
articles.summary: The tech giant said people can opt out - but privacy campaigners called it a "recipe for disaster".
articles.titleZh: Meta 允许用户从公开的 Instagram 个人资料照片制作人工智能图像，引起强烈抗议
articles.summaryZh: 这家科技巨头表示，人们可以选择退出，但隐私活动人士称这是“灾难的秘诀”。

contentParagraphs.en: Meta is facing a backlash over its new AI tool Muse Image, which can generate pictures using other people's profile pictures without telling them.It is one of many text-to-image tools publicly available, which as the name suggests can create pictures from a few lines of simple written text. Muse Image is available through the Meta AI app and web browser, as well as on WhatsApp and in Instagram Stories for US users.While Meta says users can opt out of their image being used even with a public account, Donald Campbell, advocacy director at tech justice non-profit Foxglove told the BBC it was an "obvious recipe for disaster"."We've already seen a catalogue of harms from non-consensual AI-altered images on social platforms just in the past year," he said."It is hard to see why Mark Zuckerberg thinks facilitating yet more of this creepy image manipulation is a good idea."The feature is likely to face heightened scrutiny as regulators and campaigners raise concerns about AI-generated images, with Ofcom currently investigating X over Grok's role in creating and sharing non-consensual AI-altered images of real people.Privacy International also criticised the feature, telling the BBC it was "the latest sign AI companies see people's images and data as raw material to be exploited"."Pulling real users into generated photos without explicit consent is a privacy landmine waiting to detonate," one user wrote on X, external.Meta said a dedicated setting, separate from account privacy controls, allows users to opt out even if they have a public account.To do so, users must go to Instagram's settings menu, select "Sharing and Reuse" and switch off "Allow people to reuse your content on Instagram and with AI features at Meta" for posts and reels.These settings only appear if you have a public account - if your account is private, it will already be unable to be shared.
contentParagraphs.zh: Meta 的新人工智能工具 Muse Image 正面临强烈反对，该工具可以在不告诉别人的情况下使用其他人的个人资料图片生成图片。它是众多公开可用的文本到图像工具之一，顾名思义，它可以通过几行简单的书面文本创建图片。 Muse Image 可通过 Meta AI 应用程序和网络浏览器以及 WhatsApp 和 Instagram Stories 供美国用户使用。虽然 Meta 表示，即使使用公共帐户，用户也可以选择不使用自己的图像，但技术正义非营利组织 Foxglove 的宣传总监 Donald Campbell 告诉 BBC，这是“明显的灾难根源”。“就在过去的一年里，我们已经在社交平台上看到了一系列未经同意的 AI 修改图像造成的危害，”他说。很难理解为什么马克·扎克伯格认为促进更多这种令人毛骨悚然的图像处理是一个好主意。“随着监管机构和活动人士对人工智能生成的图像提出担忧，该功能可能会面临更严格的审查，Ofcom 目前正在调查 X 因 Grok 在创建和共享未经同意的人工智能修改的真人图像方面所扮演的角色。隐私国际组织也批评了该功能，告诉 BBC，这是“人工智能公司将人的图像和数据视为原材料的最新迹象。一位用户在 X, external 上写道：“未经明确同意，将真实用户拉入生成的照片中，是一个等待引爆的隐私地雷。”Meta 表示，有一个独立于帐户隐私控制的专用设置，即使用户拥有公共帐户，也可以选择退出。为此，用户必须进入 Instagram 的设置菜单，选择“共享和重用”，并关闭帖子和卷轴的“允许人们在 Instagram 上重用你的内容，并在 Meta 上使用 AI 功能”。只有当你有公共帐户时，这些设置才会出现。帐户 - 如果您的帐户是私人帐户，则将无法共享。

articles.title: New aviation mechanics graduate with jobs in hand, thanks to a labor shortage
articles.summary: Aviation is literally soaring in the U.S., with record passenger numbers. But with a generation of mechanics set to leave the workforce, the industry needs new graduates to fill the gap.
articles.titleZh: 由于劳动力短缺，新的航空机械师毕业生手头有工作
articles.summaryZh: 美国的航空业确实在飞速发展，乘客人数创历史新高。但随着一代机械师即将离开劳动力市场，该行业需要新的毕业生来填补空缺。

contentParagraphs.en: A job forecast from Boeing says the aviation industry will need to hire 123,000 maintenance technicians in North America through 2044. Here, workers lower jacks holding up a Boeing 767-300 airplane at Newark Liberty International Airport in Newark, N.J., in 2024.
contentParagraphs.zh: 波音公司的一份就业预测显示，到 2044 年，航空业将需要在北美雇用 123,000 名维护技术人员。图中，工人们将于 2024 年在新泽西州纽瓦克自由国际机场放下千斤顶举起一架波音 767-300 飞机。

contentParagraphs.en: Angus Mordant/Bloomberg via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 安格斯·莫丹特/彭博社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Angus Mordant/Bloomberg via Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 安格斯·莫丹特/彭博社，盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: PITTSBURGH — As one graduate after another crosses the stage, cheers and applause ring out, a ritual that celebrates hard work and points to the future.
contentParagraphs.zh: 匹兹堡——当一位又一位毕业生走上讲台时，欢呼声和掌声响起，这是一种庆祝辛勤工作并指向未来的仪式。

contentParagraphs.en: For graduates in aviation maintenance at the Pittsburgh Institute of Aeronautics, it's a literal transition: After shifting their tassels and hugging their parents, they head to a nearby building for one last test.
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于匹兹堡航空学院航空维修专业的毕业生来说，这是一个真正的转变：在移动流苏并拥抱父母后，他们前往附近的一座建筑进行最后一次测试。

contentParagraphs.en: "Every one of the 54 Maintenance students took their final test graduation day or the morning after," says Derek Vrabel, the student services coordinator at PIA.
contentParagraphs.zh: “54 名维护学生中的每一位都在毕业当天或毕业后的早上参加了期末考试，”PIA 的学生服务协调员德里克·弗拉贝尔 (Derek Vrabel) 说。

contentParagraphs.en: The test isn't for a class grade. It's to earn the Federal Aviation Administration's Airframe and Powerplant (A&P) certification for mechanics to work on plane airframes and engines. The coveted credential offers a foothold in an industry eager for new hires: Even before putting on their cap and gown in late June, nearly half of the graduates had already locked in new jobs, while others were narrowing down their choices. Options range from small regional airlines to loftier aspirations.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该测试不是针对班级成绩。其目的是让机械师获得美国联邦航空管理局的机身和动力装置 (A&P) 认证，以便在飞机机身和发动机上工作。令人垂涎的证书为渴望新员工的行业提供了立足点：甚至在六月下旬戴上帽子和礼服之前，近一半的毕业生就已经锁定了新工作，而其他人则在缩小选择范围。选择范围从小型支线航空公司到更远大的志向。

contentParagraphs.en: "I do have a couple of interviews next week with a couple of contractors, and SpaceX in Texas," says class salutatorian Jon Wojcik, from Buffalo, N.Y. "I'd be applying my airframe skills for that, for the assembly of Starship rockets."
contentParagraphs.zh: 来自纽约州布法罗的班级致辞者乔恩·沃伊西克 (Jon Wojcik) 表示：“下周我确实要与几个承包商以及德克萨斯州的 SpaceX 进行几次面试。”“我将运用我的机身技能来组装星舰火箭。”

contentParagraphs.en: As for how the new graduates did on the series of oral, practical and written exams that make up the test, Vrabel says 47 of them succeeded on their first attempt. He expects the remaining seven to pass in the coming days.
contentParagraphs.zh: 至于应届毕业生在一系列口试、实践考试和笔试中的表现，弗拉贝尔表示，其中 47 人在第一次尝试中就取得了成功。他预计剩下的七个将在未来几天内通过。

contentParagraphs.en: How do aviation jobs stack up?
contentParagraphs.zh: 航空工作如何叠加？

contentParagraphs.en: Graduates stand at the Pittsburgh Institute of Aeronautics as their families and loved ones look on. The school's aviation technician program takes less than two years to complete and leads to federal certification.
contentParagraphs.zh: 毕业生们站在匹兹堡航空学院，家人和亲人在一旁观看。学校的航空技术员课程只需不到两年的时间即可完成并获得联邦认证。

contentParagraphs.en: Students have long groaned at adults' questions about what they'll do after graduation. But few grads have faced as many challenges as the current crop, such as disruptions from remote work, fears of a K-shaped economy, and the spread of AI.
contentParagraphs.zh: 长期以来，学生们一直在抱怨成年人关于毕业后做什么的问题。但很少有毕业生像现在的毕业生一样面临如此多的挑战，例如远程工作的干扰、对 K 型经济的担忧以及人工智能的传播。

contentParagraphs.en: Aviation maintenance is a rare bright spot against that grim backdrop, a field of skilled physical labor that needs a new generation of workers.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在严峻的背景下，航空维修是一个难得的亮点，这是一个需要新一代工人的熟练体力劳动领域。

contentParagraphs.en: "There's a shortage for both pilots and mechanics," Wojcik says. "All these people are retiring, I think the average age is 57, of mechanics."
contentParagraphs.zh: “飞行员和机械师都短缺，”沃伊西克说。 “所有这些机械师都在退休，我认为平均年龄是 57 岁。”

contentParagraphs.en: The commercial aviation industry will need to hire 123,000 aviation maintenance technicians in North America through 2044, according to a forecast in Boeing 's widely cited Pilot and Technician Outlook. Compare that to nearly 161,000 U.S. jobs in the field as of 2024, according to the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 根据波音公司广泛引用的《飞行员和技术人员展望》中的预测，到 2044 年，商业航空业将需要在北美雇佣 123,000 名航空维修技术人员。相比之下，根据美国劳工统计局的数据，截至 2024 年，美国该领域有近 161,000 个工作岗位。

contentParagraphs.en: Many of the awaiting jobs promise good wages. The median salary for an aviation technician was $79,140 in 2024, according to federal data. That's $30,000 above the median wages for all jobs — quite a leap for a program that takes less than two years to complete and costs about $42,000 at PIA.
contentParagraphs.zh: 许多等待的工作都承诺提供丰厚的工资。根据联邦数据，到 2024 年，航空技术员的平均工资为 79,140 美元。这比所有工作的平均工资高出了 30,000 美元——对于 PIA 需要不到两年时间完成、成本约为 42,000 美元的项目来说，这是一个相当大的飞跃。

contentParagraphs.en: 'I want to do hands-on'
contentParagraphs.zh: “我想亲自动手”

contentParagraphs.en: "I've wanted to do this since I was about 6 years old," says PIA student Kira Friedel of Indiana, Pa., who's halfway through the aviation maintenance program. Friedel says her father's interest in history and World War II led to lots of museum visits -- where she peppered technicians with questions about how they restored vintage planes.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我从大约 6 岁起就想这样做，”来自宾夕法尼亚州印第安纳州的 PIA 学生基拉·弗里德尔 (Kira Friedel) 说道，她正在完成航空维修项目的一半。弗里德尔说，她父亲对历史和第二次世界大战的兴趣导致她多次参观博物馆，在那里她向技术人员提出了有关如何修复老式飞机的问题。

contentParagraphs.en: Learning about schools like PIA during a college fair at her high school was a revelation and fed into her love for shop work, Friedel says. So when she graduated last year, she headed to Pittsburgh.
contentParagraphs.zh: 弗里德尔说，在高中的一次大学博览会上了解了像 PIA 这样的学校是一种启示，也激发了她对商店工作的热爱。所以当她去年毕业时，她去了匹兹堡。

contentParagraphs.en: "I was like, 'Oh my God, I want to do this.' I knew college wasn't my thing, but I want to do hands-on, definitely."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我当时想，‘天啊，我想这么做。’我知道大学不是我的菜，但我绝对想亲自动手。”

contentParagraphs.en: "I'm the first person in my family right now that's in a trade school," Friedel says. "My dad does IT and my mom's a nurse."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我是家里第一个在贸易学校上学的人，”弗里德尔说。 “我爸爸是做 IT 的，我妈妈是护士。”

contentParagraphs.en: Other students switched to aviation after studying somewhere else. There's a handful of veterans among the grads. And some are using trade school to trade careers. That includes Nancy Weaver.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其他学生在其他地方学习后转向航空。毕业生中不乏退伍军人。有些人正在利用贸易学校来交换职业。其中包括南希·韦弗。

contentParagraphs.en: "I graduated a while back for film, and then I decided it wasn't for me," she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我不久前从电影专业毕业，然后我决定这不适合我，”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: At her new school, Weaver found that handling sheet metal was, well … riveting.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在她的新学校，韦弗发现处理金属板非常……引人入胜。

contentParagraphs.en: "I was expecting to like working on engines, but I really like doing the sheet metal," Weaver says moments after she graduated.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我本来希望喜欢在发动机上工作，但我真的很喜欢做钣金，”韦弗毕业后不久说道。

contentParagraphs.en: The Canton, Ohio, native has a job offer in hand from Kalitta Air in Michigan. She says she originally wanted to be closer to home but now wants to branch out.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这位土生土长的俄亥俄州坎顿人手头有来自密歇根州卡利塔航空公司的工作机会。她说她原本想离家近一点，但现在想拓展业务。

contentParagraphs.en: "I mean, after all, I'm working in aircraft. Why not just hop on a plane and go back when I need to?" she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我的意思是，毕竟我是在飞机上工作。为什么不直接跳上飞机，在需要的时候就回去呢？”她说。

contentParagraphs.en: Seeing a positive return on students' investment
contentParagraphs.zh: 看到学生的投资获得积极回报

contentParagraphs.en: PIA was founded in 1929; the 60 graduates this year focused on two main programs: maintenance and electronics.
contentParagraphs.zh: PIA成立于1929年；今年的 60 名毕业生专注于两个主要课程：维护和电子。

contentParagraphs.en: The program combines classroom theory along with practical work on machinery and a fleet of older planes used for practice at an air field on Pittsburgh's outskirts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该项目将课堂理论与机械和用于匹兹堡郊区机场练习的旧飞机机队的实际工作结合起来。

contentParagraphs.en: The class of 2026 is one of PIA's largest. At least 15 employers, from American Airlines in Pennsylvania to GE Aerospace in Indiana, have already snapped up new graduates. And many new hires will likely continue to move around, according to PIA Executive Vice President Steven Sabold.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 届学生是 PIA 规模最大的班级之一。从宾夕法尼亚州的美国航空公司到印第安纳州的通用电气航空航天公司，至少有 15 家雇主已经抢购了应届毕业生。 PIA 执行副总裁 Steven Sabold 表示，许多新员工可能会继续流动。

contentParagraphs.en: "Approximately 36% of our graduates end up starting their first job in a regional air carrier like Envoy, Piedmont, Republic Airways," he says. "And then after two or three years, [they] move on and work with a major air carrier."
contentParagraphs.zh: “大约 36% 的毕业生最终在 Envoy、Piedmont、Republic Airways 等支线航空公司开始了第一份工作，”他说。 “然后两三年后，[他们]继续与一家大型航空公司合作。”

contentParagraphs.en: The 2026 graduating class of 60 at PIA is one of the school's largest.
contentParagraphs.zh: PIA 2026 届毕业生共有 60 名，是该校规模最大的毕业生之一。

contentParagraphs.en: There are about 220 aviation mechanic schools around the country, according to the FAA. They range from speci
contentParagraphs.zh: 据 FAA 称，全国约有 220 所航空机械学校。它们的范围从规格

articles.title: Amber heat health alerts in effect as UK set for one of longest-lasting heatwaves since 1976
articles.summary: Temperatures are set to rise as high as 36C (97F) for parts of southern England this week.
articles.titleZh: 英国将迎来自 1976 年以来持续时间最长的热浪之一，黄色热浪健康警报生效
articles.summaryZh: 本周英格兰南部部分地区的气温预计将升至 36 摄氏度（97 华氏度）。

contentParagraphs.en: Parts of the south and east officially entered a heatwave on Monday, after three days at or above their threshold temperatures.Heatwave criteria were met more widely on Tuesday as temperatures peaked at 32.4C in both Teddington, Richmond upon Thames, and also in Frittenden, Kent.On Wednesday the top temperature was 33.7C at Heathrow.More locations could reach 34C or 35C in the coming days, with the hot spot likely to be in southern England.Heat will continue to build and extend north and west into Northern Ireland and Scotland until Friday. Here the peak is expected in the high 20s Celsius.As high pressure shifts towards the end of the week, an easterly wind will pick up. As a result the focus of 30C will move and temperatures will dip a little in eastern areas of the UK, but many will remain in heatwave conditions. The focus of the heat will then be further west, across central southern England in particular. South Wales could also see highs of 33C on Friday.
